title: calamagrostis pseudophragmites subsp. persica
intro: Calamagrostis pseudophragmites subsp. persica, also known as Persian bentgrass, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Asia, characterized by its slender stems and feathery plumes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming Persian bentgrass has been linked to various health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved digestion, as well as potential antioxidant effects. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
